title: Lower Trust Closeout Audit
goal: Reconcile lower-trust issue closeouts from the no-active-plan implementation run.
Give future agents a compact evidence map before existing roadmap lanes are closed.
scope: Adjacent closed issues reviewed because they were part of the same planning period or roadmap lanes: #287, #288, #290, #280, and #282.
Live GitHub state checked on 2026-04-26 with `gh issue view` and `gh issue list --state all`.
non_goals: Do not close existing roadmap lanes from this audit.
Do not implement follow-up issues here.
Do not store full GitHub history inside planning state.

review_mode.review question: Which lower-trust closed issues are fully evidenced, which are bounded slices, which are covered by open follow-up, and which need reopening?
review_mode.default finding cap: none; all audited issues get a compact classification
review_mode.inputs inspected first: agentic-workspace preflight/summary, #324/#325/#323/#320 bodies, live issue state, recent commits named in #324, planning roadmap state, archived machine-first execplan

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace preflight --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; gh issue list --state all --limit 400 --json number,title,state,closedAt,url,labels; gh issue view 324 --json number,title,body,state,url; gh issue view 325 --json number,title,body,state,url; gh issue view 323 --json number,title,body,state,url; gh issue view 320 --json number,title,body,state,url; gh issue view 261/275/278/279/306/287/288/290 --json number,title,state,closedAt,url; git log --oneline --decorate --max-count=40; git show --stat --oneline d46d689 46176cf 7e87cfb 1498c7f a691a33 4c05190 fabc9b3 997f4ae e956063 8709087 1b7a947 0f3882e 32f256b 9ab677b
review_method.evidence sources: GitHub issue state, recent commit stats, `.agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml`, `.agentic-workspace/planning/external-intent-evidence.json`, `.agentic-workspace/planning/finished-work-evidence.json`, `.agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/archive/machine-first-planning-chain-closeout-2026-04-24.plan.json`

classification_legend.fully_satisfied_with_evidence: The issue's own acceptance target appears satisfied by code, contracts, tests, docs, and/or checked-in planning evidence.
classification_legend.bounded_slice_satisfied_parent_open: A concrete slice landed, but the larger lane remains open in roadmap state or parent issue state.
classification_legend.covered_by_open_followup: The closed issue has residual intent, but an open issue now owns that residual work.
classification_legend.premature_or_needs_reopening: The audit found material residual intent with no open owner.

issue_classifications.title: Reopen execplan modernization so machine-readable planning state becomes canonical and human views become derived
issue_classifications.evidence: Archived machine-first closeout records canonical execplan sidecars, typed references, and proportional review retention; commit e956063 exposed machine-first planning summary state.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #323, #324, and #325 remain open under the machine-first-planning-state roadmap lane.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Do not close the machine-first-planning-state lane from #261 alone.

issue_classifications.title: Add a multi-level structured reference model so execplans and higher-level planning surfaces can point explicitly to relevant issues, files, ranges, URLs, images, and other artifacts
issue_classifications.evidence: Archived machine-first closeout records typed references on execution and roadmap surfaces; current planning summary projects structured references, and planning tests cover execplan, handoff, and roadmap reference normalization.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the bounded structured-reference slice.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Structured references are treated as delivered for the machine-first lane closeout.

issue_classifications.title: Define proportional retention for planning review artifacts so review history stays useful without becoming a parallel archive
issue_classifications.evidence: Archived machine-first closeout records explicit proportional review-retention policy in canonical review records and compact review residue projections; review README/template now carry retention guidance.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the bounded review-retention slice.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Review retention is treated as delivered for the machine-first lane closeout.

issue_classifications.title: Slim the shipped root AGENTS entrypoint so installs converge on the thin startup contract
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 9ab677b slimmed AGENTS.md and related bootstrap adapter surfaces.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #281, #319, #326, and #230 remain open for product-residue and adapter-refresh pressure.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Treat root AGENTS slimming as satisfied, not the whole product-compression lane.

issue_classifications.title: Replace markdown-first workflow contracts with a stricter, composable workflow definition format
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 0f3882e added workflow_definition_format contracts, schema, tooling validation, and CLI surface checks.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #327 remains open for default-branch workflow posture risk.

issue_classifications.title: Take relevant system intent into explicit account when shaping slices and validating intent satisfaction
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 1b7a947 added system intent alignment fields to planning records and template/test coverage.
issue_classifications.follow_up: The roadmap keeps system-intent-operational-follow-through open until post-audit verification decides whether another small slice is needed.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Do not infer the system-intent lane is closed until that post-audit check is made.

issue_classifications.title: Add a local-only agent integration area
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit ccd2cd3 added the local-only integration area boundary and the live execplan records root, convention, and validation proof.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Roadmap keeps local-only-integration-area visible pending this audit's lane decision.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Adjacent item; included so the lane is not silently treated as closed.

issue_classifications.title: Define the first local integration root and boundary rules
issue_classifications.evidence: The active local-only integration execplan records `.agentic-workspace/local/integrations/`, vendor/runtime subfolders, ignore rules, config/defaults output, and focused validation.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the bounded root/convention slice.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Supports #287 but does not by itself close every future local-only integration question.

issue_classifications.title: Add preparatory refinement templates for strong general-purpose reasoning
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 8709087 added `.agentic-workspace/planning/pre-ingestion-refinement.md` and package/bootstrap copies.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Roadmap keeps pre-ingestion-refinement-templates visible until post-audit coverage review.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Template slice satisfied; lane closure still awaits coverage decision.

issue_classifications.title: Review friction: reduce competing first-contact surfaces
issue_classifications.evidence: Compact startup/preflight/summary queries are now the default route and 9ab677b slimmed the root adapter; current AGENTS.md points into compact package surfaces.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Graceful-partial-compliance roadmap lane remains open until the audit decides whether more generic-agent compliance work is needed.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Do not close the graceful partial-compliance lane solely from first-contact simplification.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: diff-path-based proof selector command
issue_classifications.evidence: Proof-selection behavior is now represented in contract data by commit 7e87cfb and exercised by workspace CLI tests from the compact startup/proof work.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the bounded proof-selector proposal; future proof automation can still appear as new work.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: diff-path-based proof selector command
issue_classifications.evidence: Duplicate/earlier proof-selector item; the implemented path is represented by #293 and contract proof-selection work.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #293 carries the satisfied bounded proposal; no separate reopening needed.

issue_classifications.title: Review friction: adapter vs canonical surface ambiguity
issue_classifications.evidence: Root/package AGENTS adapters now route to structured package surfaces, and summary/preflight expose the canonical active planning record.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #281 and #319 remain open for compatibility artifact replacement and adapter refresh.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Partial-compliance and product-compression lanes remain visible.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: minimum-safe-read startup command
issue_classifications.evidence: `agentic-workspace preflight --format json` and `agentic-workspace summary --format json` now provide compact startup and active-state guidance; this audit used them successfully before raw planning reads.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the minimum-safe-read slice.

issue_classifications.title: Review friction: package boundary confusion
issue_classifications.evidence: Extraction/discovery contracts and package-local AGENTS adapters distinguish source, package payload, and install boundaries.
issue_classifications.follow_up: The graceful-partial-compliance lane stays open until post-audit validation decides whether boundary confusion remains for generic agents.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: cheap-implementer mode for bounded agents
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit d46d689 added bounded implementer context to CLI/contracts/tests and package adapter instructions.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for the bounded implementer context slice.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: extract CLI command and option manifests
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 and commit deb6bee extracted command/parser/policy manifests; later commit fabc9b3 completed operation-contract coverage.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded extraction proposal.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Parent #258 remains open for broader declarative portability.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: extract module registry metadata from Python
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 and contract inventory/module-registry extraction work moved stable metadata into contract payloads.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded extraction proposal.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: extract CLI policy payloads
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 and subsequent manifest-backed CLI policy extraction moved stable policy payloads out of Python-only authorship.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded extraction proposal.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: extract setup findings and preflight policy
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 plus later contract inventory work extracted setup/preflight policy surfaces and validation.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded extraction proposal.

issue_classifications.title: Direction / proposal: extract workspace surface and path manifests
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 and manifest-backed extraction moved workspace/path manifest data into checked-in contracts.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded extraction proposal.

issue_classifications.title: Extract declarative workspace contracts
issue_classifications.live_state: merged pull request, not an issue
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #304 merged the manifest-backed command/parser/policy extraction that several issue slices depend on.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Use PR #304 as evidence, not as a closed issue requiring lane accounting.

issue_classifications.title: Define implementation-independent operation contracts
issue_classifications.evidence: Commits 0ce021e through 0259889 established operation contract schemas, registries, and validation; later commit fabc9b3 expanded command coverage.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Parent #309/#258 remains open for broader extraction posture.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Operation-contract slice satisfied; declarative-portability lane remains.

issue_classifications.title: Add black-box conformance contracts for generated tools
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 32f256b added `src/agentic_workspace/conformance.py`, conformance contracts, schemas, and generated-tool conformance tests.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Roadmap keeps generated-tool-conformance open until post-audit decides whether adapter coverage or negative cases are enough.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Do not close the generated-tool-conformance lane yet.

issue_classifications.title: Distill durable lane learning before archiving execplans
issue_classifications.evidence: Machine-first archived plans record closeout/learning fields, but the no-active-plan run and archive cleanup bug showed the process is not yet trustworthy enough.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #323, #324, and #325 directly own cleanup, audit, and reconciliation.

issue_classifications.title: Procedural operation contracts
issue_classifications.live_state: merged pull request, not an issue in the audited set
issue_classifications.evidence: PR #308 merged procedural operation-contract validation after PR #304.
issue_classifications.follow_up: Evidence item only.

issue_classifications.title: Complete Python-consumable contract extraction
issue_classifications.evidence: Commits 46176cf, 7e87cfb, 1498c7f, a691a33, 4c05190, fabc9b3, and 997f4ae added extraction maps, schemas, loaders, templates, operation contracts, parity checks, and runtime-boundary classification.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #320 remains open for authority marker policy extraction; #258 remains open as the broader declarative-portability parent.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Python-consumable-contract-extraction remains open with #320 as first post-audit slice.

issue_classifications.title: Audit remaining Python-owned extractable contract content
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 46176cf added `python_extraction_map.json`; commit 997f4ae added `python_runtime_boundary.json` to classify remaining Python-owned behavior.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #320 for the specific remaining stable authority marker data.
issue_classifications.lane_impact: Audit slice satisfied; parent extraction lane remains because a known follow-up exists.

issue_classifications.title: Extract input/output and result schemas for Python consumption
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit a691a33 added startup and implementer output schemas with contract tooling validation.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded schema slice.

issue_classifications.title: Extract static data, text, and rendering templates from Python
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 4c05190 added `context_templates.json`, schema validation, and rewired CLI rendering to consume templates.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded template slice.

issue_classifications.title: Complete operation-contract coverage for every CLI command
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit fabc9b3 expanded operation contracts for all CLI commands and validation checks.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for operation-command coverage as audited.

issue_classifications.title: Wire Python loaders and schema-derived models for extracted contracts
issue_classifications.evidence: Commits 1498c7f and surrounding extraction work added validated consumption policy and contract_tooling loader support.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded loader slice.

issue_classifications.title: Add extraction parity checks and Python-consumed contract conformance
issue_classifications.evidence: Contract tooling checks were expanded across extraction contracts, and tests cover Python consumption/parity paths.
issue_classifications.follow_up: None for this bounded parity slice.

issue_classifications.title: Classify remaining Python-owned procedural logic after extraction
issue_classifications.evidence: Commit 997f4ae added runtime boundary classification; #320 was opened for the authority-marker policy data that remains stable but Python-authored.
issue_classifications.follow_up: #320 owns the remaining authority marker extraction gap.

lane_impacts.reason: #323 and #325 remain open after #324; #261/#307 were not enough to trust closeout hygiene.
#278 landed a contract slice, but #327 owns remaining default-branch workflow posture risk.
#328 was added after the lower-trust run and remains scheduled as its own lane.
#320 remains a concrete extraction gap and #258 remains the broader parent.
#275 was satisfied as a root adapter slice, but #281, #319, #326, and #230 remain open.
#306 landed a conformance harness, but the roadmap intentionally asks whether adapter coverage and negative cases are enough.
#279 landed alignment fields, but the roadmap asks for one verification pass before closure.
#290 landed templates, but coverage across recurring intake ambiguity still needs a decision.
#287/#288 landed the root and boundary; decide separately whether runtime examples are needed.
#292/#293/#295/#296/#297/#298 landed several improvements, but the no-active-plan run proves compliance and bypass trust still need review.

findings.title: No audited item needs immediate reopening
findings.summary: The audit found no material closed-issue intent without either landed evidence or an open follow-up.
findings.risk if unchanged: Low for issue reopening; high if lanes are closed before the follow-ups are executed.
findings.suggested action: Close #324 after this artifact lands, then continue the first roadmap lane with #323/#325.
findings.source: live GitHub issue state and checked-in planning evidence
findings.post-remediation note shape: Issue #324 can close as an audit artifact, not as roadmap lane closure.

findings.title: Several issue closures were honest bounded slices, not lane closure evidence
findings.summary: #261, #275, #278, #279, #287, #290, #292, #295, #297, and #306 should not be read as proving their whole lanes complete.
findings.evidence: The current roadmap still tracks each larger lane or corresponding open follow-up.
findings.risk if unchanged: Future agents may close lanes prematurely because individual issue closure looks stronger than the evidence supports.
findings.suggested action: Keep these lanes visible until their planned post-audit slices run.
findings.promotion trigger: when implementing the product-level reconciliation view
findings.post-remediation note shape: Compact reconciliation should expose bounded-slice versus full-lane distinction.

recommendation.promote: #324 itself is satisfied by this checked-in audit artifact once validation passes.
recommendation.defer: Do not close existing roadmap lanes yet. Continue with #323/#325 in the first lane, then later lanes in priority order.
recommendation.dismiss: No immediate reopen action from this audit.

retention.closeout shape: Keep this review artifact as the durable lower-trust closeout map until #325 provides a generated reconciliation view.
retention.trigger: Retain while roadmap lanes reference lower-trust closeout signals.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Created from #324 after promoting the audit into an active execplan and checking live GitHub issue state.
